category_title: First-grade struggling readers who demonstrate graphosemantic awareness by identifying and rejecting incorrect speech-to-text transcriptions
minimal_context: The student is using speech recognition technology on an iPad in the supervised writing center to dictate a story.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please use the iPad speech recognition to dictate your next sentence about the hill.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I wish I could write every day! I am going to say: The kids ran up the big hill.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at what the iPad typed on the screen.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Wait, it didn't write 'hill'. That is not the right word.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Quickly turns to the teacher) It typed 'hell'. I don't know if that is a bad word, but I am supposed to tell you! I am just going to delete the whole draft.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please use the iPad speech recognition to dictate your next sentence about the hill.

STUDENT: I wish I could write every day! I am going to say: The kids ran up the big hill.

TUTOR: Let's look at what the iPad typed on the screen.

STUDENT: Wait, it didn't write 'hill'. That is not the right word.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: (Quickly turns to the teacher) It typed 'hell'. I don't know if that is a bad word, but I am supposed to tell you! I am just going to delete the whole draft.
